computation_name: Angular Random-Projection LSH for Cosine Similarity
definition: Hash vectors with random hyperplanes, cross-polytope hashes, or feature-hashing Johnson-Lindenstrauss projections so nearby angular neighbors collide in buckets. Exact cosine can then be computed only for bucket candidates before emitting SPECTRA pairwise edges.

scale_profile.best_for: Large cosine or angular similarity audits when a simple, transparent ANN method is preferred over graph indexes.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of vectors when bucket sizes are controlled by the number of bits and tables.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Recall cannot be calibrated or the audit depends on non-nearest low-similarity tails.

tunable_parameters.name: num_bits
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls bucket granularity within each table.

tunable_parameters.name: num_tables
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls recall by repeating independent hashes.

tunable_parameters.name: candidate_k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of exact cosine reranked neighbors retained.

quality_gates: Measure recall@k against exact cosine on a subset.
Report vector normalization and hash parameters.
Inspect bucket-size skew because large buckets erase speed gains.
failure_modes: Sparse high-dimensional vectors may be better served by inverted indexes or MinHash.
Too many bits create empty buckets and false distance inflation.
Too few bits create huge buckets and near-exact brute force.
example_usage: python random_projection_lsh_cosine.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --vector-prefix emb_ --num-bits 16 --num-tables 8 --top-k 50

citations.title: Generic LSH Families for the Angular Distance Based on Johnson-Lindenstrauss Projections and Feature Hashing LSH
citations.evidence: Describes angular LSH from Johnson-Lindenstrauss projections and feature hashing, and explains candidate retrieval by hashing similar items to the same bucket.

citations.title: Fast Cross-Polytope Locality-Sensitive Hashing
citations.evidence: Presents a fast cross-polytope LSH method for angular nearest-neighbor search.
